title: CI-RED-004 prep: run architecture_scan (Fence Checks (Blocking)) on every PR so the fence can be required without bricking docs-only PRs
intent: Branch-protection enforcement prep. CI-RED-004 closes by making the 'Fence Checks (Blocking)' status check REQUIRED on main. A GitHub required check must report on every PR, or the PR sits permanently 'Expected — waiting' and can never merge. architecture_scan.yml was path-filtered to code paths, so it skipped docs-only PRs (SPRINTS/ledger/handoff updates are frequent), which would silently brick those merges once the fence is required. This removes the pull_request/push path filters so the fence job runs on every PR (and every push to main). The fence job is self-contained (re-verifies committed baselines in ratchet mode independent of the diff), so on a docs-only PR it runs and passes cheaply. Triggers only; no job logic changed. This does NOT by itself require the fence or close CI-RED-004 — the fence is added to required checks only after this merges and is witnessed green on a docs-only PR.
behavior_change: yes - CI trigger scope: architecture_scan.yml (jobs architecture-scan + Fence Checks (Blocking)) now runs on every pull_request and every push to main, instead of only when code paths change. No job step/logic change. Slightly more CI runs on docs-only PRs; the fence passes on them (ratchet against unchanged code).

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) .github/workflows/architecture_scan.yml: removed the pull_request 'paths:' filter and the push 'paths:' filter (push stays scoped to branches:[main]); added a comment explaining the required-check rationale. Both jobs (architecture-scan [continue-on-error], fence-checks / 'Fence Checks (Blocking)') now run on every PR and every push to main. (2) this per-PR CBSP21 manifest.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: A required status check that is path-filtered is a self-DoS: on any PR that does not touch the filtered paths (e.g. a docs-only SPRINTS/ledger PR) the workflow never runs, the required check never reports, and the PR is blocked forever on 'Expected — waiting'. Removing the filter is the prerequisite that lets the fence be required at all. It also records the blocking fence verdict on every merge commit to main (the original reason the push trigger exists), including docs-only merges that the old push filter skipped.

commands_run: python -c yaml.safe_load(architecture_scan.yml) -> triggers push/pull_request/workflow_dispatch, pull_request=None (all PRs), jobs=[architecture-scan, fence-checks]
PR #194: 'Fence Checks (Blocking)' -> pass (14s) — fence runs green under the unfiltered triggers
witness pending: 'Fence Checks (Blocking)' green on a docs-only PR before adding it to required checks

files.intent: Remove pull_request/push path filters so 'Fence Checks (Blocking)' runs on every PR and every push to main; the fence can then be a required check without bricking docs-only PRs.
files.behavior_change: yes - CI trigger scope only; fence + architecture-scan now run on every PR and push to main. No job logic change.
verification: docs-only PR witness (fence runs + passes) before requiring the check

files.intent: Per-PR CBSP21 manifest declaring this fence-trigger change.
